name: Coquilles Saint-Jacques Sautees a La Provenca
recipeIngredient: A 10 inch frying pan (preferably a non-stick pan)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dry the scallops in paper towels and place on a large sheet of waxed paper.
2. Sprinkle with drops of lemon juice, then with salt and pepper.
3. Just before sauteing, dredge the scallops in flour and shake off excess flour.
4. Coat the frying pan with 1/16-inch layer of oil.
5. When almost smoking, add the scallops to the pan.
6. Toss for 4 to 5 minutes until scallops are lightly browned on the outside.
7. Add shallots or scallions and garlic; toss briefly.
8. Finally, add butter and parsley and serve.
9. Suggested accompaniments: French bread (if scallops are a first course) or broiled tomatoes and green beans (for a main course).
Notes:
Sauteed scallops should be crisp, light brown on the outside and moistly tender inside. To achieve this, ensure the sauteing oil is very hot before adding the scallops, and avoid having more than one layer of scallops in the pan to prevent steaming and moisture release which can hinder browning. For frozen scallops, blanch them in rapidly boiling water for a moment, then quickly return to the boil and drain immediately before proceeding with the recipe.
